reasons: Tigreal can disrupt enemy positioning and create the movement-heavy fight windows Phoveus wants.
Phoveus can follow Tigreal's grouped fight after enemies are forced into bad positions.

proof.summary: Tigreal disrupts enemy positioning, while Phoveus punishes movement and displacement windows inside the same teamfight.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages before enemies spread out.
Phoveus stays close enough to benefit from movement-triggered opportunities.
failureCases: Tigreal engages outside Phoveus's follow-up range.
Enemies save mobility until after Phoveus commits.
Phoveus is kited before reaching the fight.

reasons: Atlas can force a grouped fight where enemies are more likely to use movement skills to escape.
Phoveus can capitalize on the movement chaos after Atlas starts the engage.

proof.summary: Atlas forces enemies into a grouped control window, and Phoveus punishes their movement responses during the follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Phoveus waits for enemy escape skills or displacement windows.
failureCases: Atlas catches only targets Phoveus cannot reach.
Enemy team disengages before Phoveus enters.
Phoveus commits before Atlas forces movement.

reasons: Ruby's repeated control can keep enemies close enough for Phoveus to pressure them.
Phoveus benefits from extended close-range fights where enemies repeatedly reposition or attempt to escape.

proof.summary: Ruby keeps enemies disrupted in close range, while Phoveus uses the fight duration to punish movement and re-entry attempts.
worksBestWhen: Ruby and Phoveus fight around the same target area.
Ruby holds control until Phoveus can join.
failureCases: Ruby pulls targets away from Phoveus.
Phoveus is too far to benefit from the control chain.
Enemy ranged heroes kite both fighters.

reasons: Chou can force one target into a bad position, giving Phoveus a focused follow-up target.
Phoveus benefits when Chou's pickoff creates a close-range fight instead of a scattered teamfight.

proof.summary: Chou isolates or displaces a target, and Phoveus follows the forced fight to punish movement and escape attempts.
worksBestWhen: Chou kicks a target toward Phoveus or team follow-up.
Phoveus is already near the expected landing area.
failureCases: Chou kicks the target away from Phoveus.
The target uses immunity or escape after the kick.

reasons: Jawhead can force a target into Phoveus's threat area.
Phoveus benefits when Jawhead's throw or displacement starts a committed close-range skirmish.

proof.summary: Jawhead creates a forced-position target, and Phoveus follows with movement-punish pressure.
worksBestWhen: Jawhead throws enemies toward Phoveus or team damage.
Phoveus waits for the displacement before committing.
failureCases: Jawhead throws the target away from the team.
Phoveus is not close enough to follow.
The enemy immediately disengages after the throw.
